name: fly-vscode-extension
description: Fly programming language support for Visual Studio Code: syntax highlighting, snippets, and language configuration for .fly files.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Fly
walkthroughs.description: Set up the Fly compiler, write your first program, and build and debug it.

steps.title: Select the Fly compiler
steps.description:
Run **Fly: Select Compiler** to point the extension to your Fly installation. The status bar shows the active version once found.

[Select Compiler](command:fly.selectCompiler)

steps.title: Write your first Fly program
steps.description: Create a `.fly` file and start typing — hover docs, completions, and syntax highlighting activate automatically.

steps.title: Build and run
steps.description: Press **Ctrl+F5** to compile and run, **Ctrl+Shift+B** to build only, or **F5** to launch the debugger (requires codelldb).

steps.title: Manage your project with flyp
steps.description: Open a `fly.toml` manifest to access package manager commands: **Flyp: Build**, **Flyp: Run**, **Flyp: Add Dependency**, and more.

commands.title: Flyp: Build
commands.description: Build the project (incremental — only recompiles changed targets)

commands.title: Flyp: Deploy to Registry
commands.description: Package and upload the current project to the configured registry

commands.title: Flyp: Vendor to Registry
commands.description: Push all locked git dependencies to a local registry

commands.title: Flyp: Clean
commands.description: Remove build artifacts and incremental build fingerprints, forcing a full rebuild on next build

commands.title: Flyp: Upgrade Dependencies
commands.description: Upgrade tag-pinned git dependencies to the latest semver tag

commands.title: Flyp: Doctor
commands.description: Check compiler, manifest, lockfile, cache and registries

configurationSnippets.label: Fly: Launch and debug current file
configurationSnippets.description: Compile with --debug and launch under LLDB (requires the codelldb extension).

task.description: The fly task to run (e.g. build)

fly.compilerPath.description: Path to the Fly compiler executable. Used to run live diagnostics on save.

fly.enableDiagnostics.description: Run the Fly compiler on save and show errors and warnings as inline diagnostics.

fly.lspPath.description: Path to the fly-lsp binary. Leave empty to auto-discover next to fly.compilerPath.

fly.enableLsp.description: Enable the Fly Language Server for semantic features (hover on symbols, go-to-definition, find references).

fly.buildArgs.description: Extra arguments appended to the fly compiler when running 'Fly: Build File', e.g. "-O2" or "-o bin/myapp".

fly.flypPath.description: Path to the flyp binary. Leave empty to auto-discover next to fly.compilerPath.

fly.flypProfile.description: Build profile used by the Flyp: Build / Run / Test commands (e.g. "release", "ci"). Leave empty to use the default profile (debug). Equivalent to passing --profile <name>.

fly.flypJobs.description: Number of parallel compilation threads for Flyp: Build (-j N). 0 = auto-detect CPU cores. 1 = sequential. With a single target, all threads go to the fly compiler (LLVM internal parallelism); with multiple targets, flyp runs them concurrently.

fly.flypOffline.description: Run Flyp: Build / Run / Test without network access (--offline). Uses only the local dependency cache. Fails if any locked dependency is missing from cache.

fly.flypForceRebuild.description: Run flyp clean before every Flyp: Build, bypassing the incremental build cache. Equivalent to running Flyp: Clean then Flyp: Build manually.

fly.flypCrossTriple.description: Cross-compilation target triple for Flyp: Build (e.g. "aarch64-linux-musl", "wasm32-wasi"). Leave empty for native builds. Output goes to target/<profile>/<triple>/.

fly.flypToken.description: API key sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' when running Flyp: Deploy or Flyp: Vendor. Leave empty if the registry has no authentication. Equivalent to passing --token or setting FLYP_TOKEN.

fly.flypBuildArgs.description: Extra arguments appended to flyp build, e.g. "--targets mybin,mylib". Use fly.flypProfile to select a build profile.

fly.debugBuildArgs.description: Extra compiler flags used by 'Fly: Debug File', e.g. "--debug -O0".

fly.enableWorkspaceDiagnostics.description: Scan all .fly files in the workspace for errors at startup, not only files that are explicitly opened.
